title: Coarse-grained dynamic RNA titration simulations
author: Pasquali, Samuela
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Electrostatic interactions play a pivotal role in many biomolecular processes. The 
molecular organization and function in biological systems are largely determined 
by these interactions. Due to the highly negative charge of RNA, the effect is expected to be 
more pronounced in this system. Moreover, RNA base-pairing is dependent on the charge of the base, 
giving rise to alternative secondary and tertiary structures. The equilibrium between uncharged 
and charged bases is regulated by the solution pH, which is therefore a key 
environmental condition influencing the molecule's structure and behavior. By means of constant-pH Monte Carlo 
simulations based on a fast proton titration scheme, coupled with the coarse-grained model 
HiRE-RNA, molecular dynamic simulations of RNA molecules at constant pH enable us to explore 
the RNA conformational plasticity at different pH values as well as to compute 
electrostatic properties as local pKa values for each nucleotide.
